message:
feat(viz): upgrade matrix, degree ripple, peeling, precode, and toy decode visualizations

Rework all five interactive visualizations for better clarity, animation, and
pedagogical value:

Matrix Visualization (Interactive 01 — Gaussian Pivoting):
- Remove isNew tracking on row objects; drive all entry animations through GSAP
  string selectors instead of DOM queries
- Add rotate:-45 to cell entrance animation for a more dynamic reveal
- Overhaul status display: 6xl font-black with conditional cyan glow and
  drop-shadow when full rank is reached; pulsing animation on completion
- Show "Linearly Independent" / "Dependent" label per packet row so the reader
  can see which equations actually contribute rank
- Larger cell gaps (gap-8, gap-3) and richer empty-state with dashed border
  and pulsing "Waiting for Data..." text

Degree Distribution & Ripple (Interactive 02 — Real-Time Graph Sim):
- Reduce displayed degree range from 1–30 to 1–12 (the tail beyond d=12 is
  negligible and compressed the bars into unreadable slivers)
- Simplify RFC 6330 distribution to an explicit 12-element probability vector
  matching the spec's concentration on low degrees
- Replace full bipartite-graph peeling simulation with a lighter parametric
  model: ripple is tracked as a continuous quantity with stochastic perturbation
  and a decay phase after 90% progress; this runs in O(1) per frame instead of
  O(K) and avoids freezing the UI at large K values
- Animate the ripple line with stroke-dashoffset reveal (2s cubic-in-out) so the
  chart draws itself from left to right
- Reformat stats bar with HTML spans and cyan highlight on recovery percentage

Peeling Visualization (Interactive 05 → renumbered 04 — Force-Directed):
- Increase graph size from K=8, M=12 to K=12, M=18 for a richer cascade
- Add degree-3 packets (30% probability) alongside degree-1 (20%) and degree-2
  (50%) for more realistic structure
- Replace static positioned layout with D3 force simulation (forceLink d=100,
  forceManyBody strength=-300, forceCenter) so nodes rearrange organically as
  edges are removed during peeling
- Rewrite render() to use proper enter/update/merge pattern with <g> group
  elements instead of bare circles; add stroke differentiation for solved/active
  nodes and opacity=0 for fully peeled packets
- Step logic updated to work with D3 force object references; simulation
  reheats (alpha=0.3) after each peel step

Precode Visualization (Interactive 03 → renumbered 05):
- Increase from K=24, P=4 to K=30, P=6 for better visual density
- Restructure animation stages with cleaner class naming (.rx for received
  symbols) and larger step size (14px vs 16px) for tighter packing
- Repair path now uses stroke-width 3 and 6,3 dash pattern

Toy Decode (Interactive 04):
- Shorten step descriptions to focus on the mathematical operation rather than
  narrative framing ("Packet 1 received:" vs "Recv PKT 1:")
- Enlarge symbol cards (p-6, rounded-3xl, border-2) with stronger active glow
  (30px shadow spread) for better visual hierarchy during the walkthrough
- Switch equation display from stacked list to horizontal pill layout (flex-wrap,
  rounded-full, 10px tracking) for a more compact visual
- Capitalize status line ("TRACE: STEP 1 / 6")

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
